texto: John Armato, a Public relations executive, cherishes his growing Library of Candidates. When people ask him if he's actually read all those books, he asks them if they've actually eaten all the food in their kitchen. "It is good to put up a supply of books; it increases the odds that you'll have what you want when you're hungry for it," he says

texto: In our land of opportunities and distractions, it's hard to devote our attention to the quiet pleasures of reading. It's as if we live our lives in a noisy restaurant and can't have the intimate conversation we most yearn for.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Steve Leveen: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “In our land of opportunities and distractions, it's hard to devote our attention to the quiet ple…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que vida, guide, reading pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reading e well-read.
